steps.title: Identify the form of the relationship
steps.content: Each increase of 1 in $x$ multiplies $y$ by 4 (a constant factor, not a constant addition), so the relationship is exponential with base 4. The general form is $y = a \cdot 4^x$. Since $y = 150$ when $x = 0$, the coefficient $a = 150$, giving $y = 150(4)^x$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = 150(4)^{x-1}$: At $x = 0$, this gives $y = 150(4)^{-1} = 37.5$, not 150. The initial condition fails. ✗
  • Choice B — $y = 150(4)^x$: At $x = 0$, $y = 150(4)^0 = 150$. ✓ At $x = 1$, $y = 150 \times 4 = 600$, a factor of 4 increase. ✓ Both conditions satisfied. ✓
  • Choice C — $y = 4(150)^x$: The base is 150, not 4. At $x = 1$, $y = 4 \times 150 = 600$, but at $x = 2$, $y = 4 \times 150^2 = 90{,}000$ — a factor of 150 increase, not 4. The growth factor is wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 4(150)^{x-1}$: Same base problem as C, and at $x = 0$, $y = 4(150)^{-1} \approx 0.027$, far from 150. Both conditions fail. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
